evidence: Citation block in paragraph 4: '*State v. Hamilton*, 2023 ND 233, $\P$ 10, 999 N.W.2d 214.' Source markdown has double spaces before and after the LaTeX paragraph symbol per professional formatting convention.

Citation in paragraph 28 (Section VI): 'But we may exercise our discretion to review for obvious error to ensure the integrity of judicial proceedings. *Id.* at $\P$ 12.' Source markdown has double spaces before and after the LaTeX paragraph symbol.
Malformed HTML entity with escaped '<' character. The '<' is incorrectly encoded as '&</sup>lt;' instead of being properly closed. The footnote is rendered cleanly in the PDF without this corruption.
Line 43 shows a lone 'T' as a section divider between paragraphs [¶1] and [¶2]. The PDF clearly shows a Roman numeral 'I'. The 'T' is an OCR misrecognition of the serif Roman numeral 'I'.

Line 56 shows Greek Pi (Π) as a section divider between [¶4] and [¶5]. The PDF shows plain 'II' (Roman numeral two). This is an OCR character misrecognition, likely from confusion with visual similarity of serif 'II' to the Greek character.
Line 74 shows Greek Alpha (Α) as a section divider between [¶8] and [¶9]. The PDF shows Roman numeral 'III'. The Greek character is an OCR substitution error.
Line 78 shows Cyrillic Be (В) as a section divider between [¶9] and [¶10]. The PDF shows 'IV' (Roman numeral four). This is a Cyrillic character substitution error from OCR processing.

evidence: Markdown heading syntax '####' appears before Roman numeral section headers. This is a format artifact from ingestion—the PDF shows plain Roman numerals, not markdown. Verified by parallel structure at line 172 ('### VIII' → should be 'VIII'). ND opinions do not use markdown; they use plain text section headings.
note: Format artifact from conversion process. Database contains markdown while court PDFs are plain text.

source: Database text near citation: *Guardianship of P.T.*, 2014 ND 223
evidence: LaTeX escape sequence '$\P$' is ingestion artifact for pilcrow/paragraph symbol. ND court opinions use plain pilcrow ¶. First instance in Guardianship of P.T. citation at para 8. All other pilcrows in document appear as ¶, not LaTeX notation.
note: Consistent with citation format throughout opinion; fixes notation inconsistency.

source: Database text at section VIII heading
evidence: Markdown heading syntax '###' before final section Roman numeral. Matches structure error at line 62 ('#### III'). All section headers in court opinions should be plain text without markdown. This is the final section marker before [¶37].
note: Format artifact; parallel to #### III fix. Entire section heading system appears to have been corrupted through markdown formatting during ingestion.

source: PDF pdftotext extraction at paragraph 8, rule citation block
evidence: The PDF source shows 'Rule 58 of the North Dakota Supreme Court Administrative Rules "addresses vexatious' on one line, followed by page break (page number '2'), then 'litigation."' on the next line. The OCR/ingestion process incorrectly inserted an 'A' between 'vexatious' and 'litigation', likely picking up a stray character or section header artifact from the PDF layout. The PDF clearly shows these two words belong together with no 'A' between them.
Per Rule 8: Spaced ellipsis is ingestion artifact. PDF shows compact form. This is in quoted Rule 59(b) statute in paragraph 6, at end of the quoted rule text.
